questions: Q1: Were all CLI implementation steps completed?
Q2: Does hxFetch maintain backward compatibility?

answers.answer: Yes, all 6 steps completed: hxFetch generalized with basePath, comments dispatcher created, list and post commands implemented, comments registered in CLI entry point, and quality gates pass.
evidence: src/lib/http.ts line 40: basePath option added with default /api/inspect
src/comments/index.ts: runComments dispatches list and post

answers.answer: Yes, the basePath parameter defaults to '/api/inspect' which is the existing path. All existing callers (inspect commands) continue to work unchanged since they don't pass basePath. Only the new comments commands pass basePath: '/api'.

statement: All CLI changes implemented: hxFetch generalized with backward-compatible basePath, comments list/post commands created, CLI entry point updated. Zero new runtime dependencies. Typecheck and build pass.
